trade.summary: Saudi Arabia is Japan's largest single source of crude oil, accounting for roughly 40% of Japan's oil imports. This energy dependency fundamentally shapes the bilateral relationship. Japan exports automobiles (Toyota, Nissan, Honda dominate Saudi market), machinery, and electronics to Saudi Arabia. Trade is highly asymmetric, dominated by Saudi hydrocarbon exports.

diplomatic.summary: Japan-Saudi relations are primarily driven by energy security imperatives. Japan maintains careful neutrality on Middle Eastern political disputes to preserve hydrocarbon supply. Saudi Arabia is interested in Japanese technology for Vision 2030 diversification. High-level exchanges are regular. Japan has largely abstained from criticizing Saudi human rights practices to protect energy ties.

military.summary: No formal military alliance. Japan maintains strict policies against arms exports, though these have been relaxed in recent years. No significant arms transfers to Saudi Arabia. Japan's constitutional limitations on overseas military deployments prevent meaningful defense cooperation. Japan does coordinate on maritime security in the Persian Gulf region to protect its oil tanker routes.

financial.summary: Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund (PIF) has made significant investments in Japanese technology and entertainment companies. Saudi Aramco has strategic partnerships with Japanese refiners (ENEOS, Idemitsu). Japanese trading companies (Mitsui, Mitsubishi, JXTG) have joint ventures in Saudi petrochemical projects. Vision 2030 has attracted Japanese infrastructure investment interest.

energy.summary: Saudi Arabia is the cornerstone of Japan's energy security. Saudi Aramco supplies approximately 38-42% of Japan's crude oil imports under long-term contracts. The two countries also cooperate on hydrogen energy, with Saudi Arabia positioning itself as a future hydrogen exporter to Japan. This energy dependency is Japan's most critical bilateral energy relationship.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific cooperation aligned with Saudi Vision 2030's science and technology ambitions. Joint research in renewable energy, desalination, and petrochemical engineering. Japanese universities have partnerships with Saudi institutions including KAUST. Academic exchanges are expanding.
